tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Six Years After Fractured Skull, Raúl Jiménez Scores Emotional World Cup Goal
stories.summary: Six years after a fractured skull nearly ended his life, Raúl Jiménez wore his protective headband and nearly scored Mexico's second goal at the Azteca stadium, 80,000 spectators groaning as one.

stories.headline: Goalkeeper's Miraculous Return From Career-Threatening Injury Seals South Korea's World Cup Victory
stories.summary: Kim Seung-gyu, who nearly retired after major injury a year ago, made miraculous stoppage-time saves to secure South Korea's 2-1 comeback victory over Czech Republic.

stories.headline: Woman's Kindness: Water Bottles Left for Delivery Workers in Summer Heat
stories.summary: For two years, a woman known as @thoughtfully_designed refills water bottles outside her door each morning for delivery partners from Swiggy, Zomato, Amazon, and others braving the heat.

stories.headline: Messi Pauses Celebration to Give Full Attention to Venezuelan Journalist With Cerebral Palsy
stories.summary: Venezuelan journalist Manu Gutiérrez, who uses a wheelchair and has cerebral palsy, realized his years-long dream interviewing Lionel Messi, now 38 years old, after Argentina's World Cup warm-up match.

stories.headline: Reserve Player Scores World Cup Winner Despite 38-Degree Fever, Fulfilling Four-Year Dream
stories.summary: Oh Hyeon-gyu, fighting through a fever nearing 40 degrees, scored South Korea's winning goal four years after being a reserve without a jersey number, finally fulfilling his World Cup dream.

stories.headline: Lost Amasya Tulip Rediscovered After 130 Years in Türkiye
stories.summary: A nature enthusiast's keen eyes reunited botanists with an endemic tulip species thought extinct for 130 years, discovered alive and flourishing in Türkiye.

stories.headline: Belmont Resident Rallies Community to Build Fridge and Pantry Against Food Insecurity
stories.summary: Amber Porter unites neighbors and local groups to create a community fridge and pantry, addressing food insecurity affecting over 700 households.

stories.headline: Architect Walks 1,600km From Durban to Cape Town, Discovering Hidden Kindness and Housing Solutions
stories.summary: Wandile Mthiyane walked across South Africa sharing meals with strangers, raising awareness about the housing crisis while discovering communities' remarkable generosity.

stories.headline: Endangered Nilgiri Tahrs Rebound From Zero to 26 in Two Years Across Indian Mountain Habitats
stories.summary: Nilgiri Tahrs rebounded from zero to 26 in just two years across Coimbatore's fragmented mountain habitats, reviving hope for India's endangered state animal.

stories.headline: Nearly 200 Endangered American Burying Beetles Released Into Ohio After 40-Year Absence
stories.summary: Nearly 200 American burying beetles with orange antennas were released into Ohio after 40 years of absence, marking the triumphant return of a species once common across 35 states.

stories.headline: Cheap Iron Replaces Platinum in Hydrogen Fuel Cells, Setting World Durability Record
stories.summary: Engineers replaced expensive platinum with iron in hydrogen fuel cells, achieving a durability record that could make clean energy affordable for commercial fleets.

stories.headline: Restaurant Oyster Shells Rebuild California's Lost Oyster Reefs and Coastal Ecosystems
stories.summary: Orange County restaurants' discarded oyster shells are rebuilt into foundations for native Olympia oyster reefs, restoring California's lost coastal ecosystems one curing pile at a time.

stories.headline: Solar-Powered Reactor Transforms CO₂ and Farm Waste Into Valuable Chemicals
stories.summary: University of Nottingham's solar reactor converts greenhouse gases into useful chemicals while turning farm waste into sustainable plastic building blocks, achieving 93% efficiency.

stories.headline: MIT Engineers Develop Chest Sticker That Regulates Heartbeats Using Ultrasound
stories.summary: A noninvasive pacemaker sticker uses ultrasound to regulate heartbeats without surgery, successfully tested in rats with potential for human patients.
